questions.question: Find the odd one out among the following numbers: 25, 16, 65, 9
questions.explanation: 9, 16, and 25 are perfect squares. 65 is not a perfect square.
questions.trick: Memorize squares up to 30 to instantly spot discrepancies.

questions.question: Which state emerged as the overall champion in the 6th edition of the Khelo India Youth Games 2023, held from January 19 to February 1, 2024?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra topped the medal tally with 158 medals (57 gold, 48 silver, 53 bronze) at the Khelo India Youth Games 2023, held in Tamil Nadu.
questions.trick: Host state was Tamil Nadu, but the winner was Maharashtra, continuing its dominance in these games.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: At about 6,650 km long, it flows into the Mediterranean Sea.
questions.trick: Amazon is largest by volume, Nile is longest by length.

questions.question: If BAT is coded as DCV, how will PEN be coded in that same language?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by exactly 2 positions in the English alphabet.
questions.trick: Note the position gap between the first letter of the word and the first letter of its code.

questions.question: Who has been recently appointed as the Chairperson of the Lokpal of India in February 2024?
questions.explanation: Justice Ajay Manikrao Khanwilkar, a former Supreme Court judge, was appointed as the new Chairperson of the Lokpal of India in February 2024.
questions.trick: Remember 'Khanwilkar' for Lokpal, distinct from the Chief Justice of India.

questions.question: In which year did Mahatma Gandhi return to India from South Africa?
questions.explanation: Gandhi returned to India on Jan 9, 1915 (Pravasi Bharatiya Divas).
questions.trick: Remember 1915 as the start of his Indian movements.

questions.question: What will be the Simple Interest on a principal amount of Rs. 17600 at an interest rate of 12% per annum over 2 years?
What is the name of the new scheme launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in February 2024, aiming to provide free electricity through rooftop solarization?
options: PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana
questions.correct_answer: PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana
questions.explanation: PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana was launched in February 2024 with an outlay of over Rs 75,000 crore to provide free electricity to 1 crore households through rooftop solarization.
questions.trick: The name directly translates to 'PM Sun Home Free Electricity Scheme'.

questions.question: Rs. 432 is divided between A and B in the ratio 4:5. What is A's share?
questions.explanation: Total parts = 4 + 5 = 9. Value of 1 part = 432/9 = 48. A's share = 4 * 48 = 192.
questions.trick: Divide total by sum of ratios, then multiply by A's ratio digit.

questions.question:
Statements:
1. All Pens are Pencils.
2. All Pencils are Stationery.

Which conclusion logically follows?
questions.explanation: If A is a subset of B, and B is a subset of C, then A must be a subset of C completely.
questions.trick: Draw a venn diagram: Small circle A inside Medium B inside Large C.

questions.question: A can complete a piece of work in 48 days while B can complete the same work in 64 days. If they work together, in how many days will they complete the work?
questions.explanation: Work = 48 * 64 = 3072. Rate = 48 + 64 = 112. Time = Work / Rate = 27.43 days.

questions.question: How many individuals were posthumously or otherwise conferred with India's highest civilian honour, Bharat Ratna, by the Indian government in February 2024?
questions.explanation: In February 2024, the government announced Bharat Ratna for five distinguished personalities: P. V. Narasimha Rao, Chaudhary Charan Singh, M. S. Swaminathan (all posthumous), along with Karpoori Thakur (posthumous) and L. K. Advani.
questions.trick: Remember the specific number 'five' as it was an unusually high number of awards given in a single year.

questions.question: Which specific payment bank was recently put under severe restrictions by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in January 2024, preventing it from accepting new deposits or top-ups?
questions.explanation: The RBI imposed severe restrictions on Paytm Payments Bank Ltd in January 2024, prohibiting it from accepting new deposits or top-ups in any customer accounts, wallets, FASTags, etc., after February 29, 2024 (later extended to March 15, 2024) due to persistent non-compliance and supervisory concerns.
questions.trick: The issue was widely covered in news; associate 'Paytm' with 'RBI restrictions'.

questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 19, 49, 79, 109, 139, ?
questions.explanation: The series follows an Arithmetic Progression with a constant difference of 30. So, 139 + (30) = 169.
questions.trick: Always check the difference between the first and second terms and verify across the series.

questions.question: A train travels at an average speed of 66 km/hr. How much distance will it cover in 2 hours?
questions.trick: Basic multiplication rule: D = S * T.

questions.question: Which is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: It covers more than 30% of the Earth's surface.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars is covered in iron oxide (rust) dust.

questions.question: Who was appointed as the new Director General (DG) of the Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB) in January 2024?
questions.explanation: IPS officer Daljit Singh Chaudhary was appointed as the new Director General of Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB) in January 2024. He also holds an additional charge as DG of NSG.
questions.trick: Many DG appointments happened around this time; Chaudhary for SSB.
